description: <onTransition to=.X> cross-state effect vs same-state divergence (§51.0.H Form 2 + §51.0.S.3 + §51.0.F.1). Two .Tick dispatches resolve .Playing (same-state): each RUNS its arm-body effect (@score++) but does NOT fire <onTransition to=.Title>. A .Quit dispatch resolves .Title (cross-state): it fires <onTransition> exactly once. Final: score 2 (both ticks' effects), transitions 1 (only the real transition), engine Title.
rationale: §51.0.S.3 a same-state arm still runs its body effect (score increments on each Tick), but §51.0.F.1 a self-target resolves to no real transition, so <onTransition> stays closed for the Ticks. §51.0.H Form 2 the cross-state .Quit (Playing -> Title) is a real transition and fires <onTransition to=.Title> once.
